title: Rebuild by the Numbers split
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild by the Numbers [split]             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Sat Dec 04, 2010 4:28 am Split from here . LiLi wrote: I guess by the same logic NGE would have to "suck" too, since it could be argued that in series Rei's character was botched, especially her relationship with Shinji given how her character development suddenly "got shafted" as soon as Asuka made her grand entrance in ep.8.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 04, 2010 4:43 am 1731298478 wrote: I think this is an interesting point. Somehow, I've always felt that there was a certain ... coldness in NGE's treatment of Rei. I've often wondered, as I'm sure others have, whether Anno and the other writers ever really liked this character. She seemed destined to lead a hopeless existence. The spectacle of Rei "finally being allowed to return" and becoming Lilith... I think it's a very sublime image, but because it is sublime very brutal. Still, this "distance," this "hopelessness" is a part of "Rei," and I don't in any sense fault NGE for it. As Tsurumaki said about EoE: "I don't think we lied." For that reason, I was more than surprised - I was completely shocked - by the ending of the second "Rebuild" film. Not for one second during the final sequence did I believe Shinji would save Rei. I remember flinching when the skin came off of Shinji's hand, and thinking how brutal it was to extend the sequence for so long... I still don't know what to think about the fact that Shinji finally "saved" her. But when Rei looked up, I had some strange feeling about her as a "forgotten" character. I think the expression Rei wore at the end was amazingly appropriate - not a smile, just this blank stare ahead, as though she was shell-shocked. Of course, Rei remains a "tool" used to initiate Third Impact, and I'm afraid she will suffer at the hands of Kaworu (if she isn't absorbed into Yui or something), so I'm not surprised she didn't smile. But it was a very interesting, very intense moment. Anyway, that sequence, and the film on the whole, touched off in me very strange emotions. I certainly understand, at least to a certain extent, the antipathy towards the film on account of its treatment of Asuka. In addition, I found myself considerably upset reading Enokido's "Asuka-Mari trading places" scenario. But Eva always upsets me anyway... ^^ I suppose I feel, with Asuka, that there is always hope for her, no matter how much she suffers, and that in addition she has taken a considerable step forward as far as her relationship with Shinji is concerned (recognizing her feelings for him, which never explicitly happened before). For Rei, I don't think even a temporary "salvation" can alter her ultimate destiny, and I don't think "Shinji-Rei" was ever a "real" possibility, for a number of reasons (I don't think S/R is in real competition with S/A, although S/R and S/K may well be similar enough "relationships" to lead to Kaworu-Rei problems). I suppose we'll see what happens, as Anno has put himself in a position where all paths are open. But for the moment I find myself worried for, and anticipating the future of, both Asuka and Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Dec 04, 2010 4:49 am 1731298478 wrote: I've often wondered, as I'm sure others have, whether Anno and the other writers ever really liked this character.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Dec 04, 2010 4:59 am @Hyper Shinchan, you pretty much summed up my thoughts and quabbles with NGE as well as my hopes and fears of Rebuild I really want to see Rei get a happy ending in Rebuild, but at the same time I also fear that they will just have her end up the same way in the series, yet at the same time i'm not exactly expecting a R/S shipping at the end either once (and if) Rei becomes her own individual, she and Shinji might eventually see that they're probably not meant for each other romantically and simply remain friends same with any shipping as well, since as teens grow up, their romantic feelings towards certain individuals may diminish (or increase, in some cases), so that's a possibility too all in all, I just want to see Rei live (and Mari be her own individual. If it turns out that she's just a carbon copy of Asuka Soryu or even really is Asuka Soryu, I would find that to be utter bullshit, since making a new character and have her turn out to be just another old character is pointless. its amusing in Wrestling, but not here :P)

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sat Dec 04, 2010 1:39 pm 1731298478 wrote: I think this is an interesting point. Somehow, I've always felt that there was a certain ... coldness in NGE's treatment of Rei. I've often wondered, as I'm sure others have, whether Anno and the other writers ever really liked this character. She seemed destined to lead a hopeless existence. The spectacle of Rei "finally being allowed to return" and becoming Lilith... I think it's a very sublime image, but because it is sublime very brutal. Still, this "distance," this "hopelessness" is a part of "Rei," and I don't in any sense fault NGE for it. As Tsurumaki said about EoE: "I don't think we lied."

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Dec 04, 2010 4:53 pm ran1 wrote: @Hyper-Shinchan : caught your remark about my concept of a Rebuild-hate team. Once again, you taking that seriously is more proof that you cannot handle forum discussion. Obvious meta-trolling was obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Dec 04, 2010 10:31 pm So my question is, if nobody's convinced anyone of anything, why does anybody bother to continue debating? Is it fun? Do the people involved feel like they'll "lose" if they back down? Is anyone still hoping to convince others that they're more correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:44 pm Just for lulz: Honestly, honestly think of how you would have reacted if Rebuild had come first and they had served you the original Series later. I bet you'd be calling it 'fanfic' too.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:46 pm Kendrix wrote: Just for lulz: Honestly, honestly think of how you would have reacted if Rebuild had come first and they had served you the original Series later. I bet you'd be calling it 'fanfic' too.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:48 pm Kendrix wrote: Just for lulz

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:51 pm Hmm this is the more OT place for me to have made comments recently such as the one about merch taking priority over character and plot. Apologies to the subforum might be in order. I long enjoyed and often defended the Rebuild films, most notably 2.22. But so much of this was predicated on the faith I had that there was vision, story, foresight, meaning, and planning involved with this enterprise. I honestly couldn't, or refused to, believe that this was going to be such a seat-of-the-pants exercise. The only way I could quell misgivings about the films and characterizations was that 'this is happening for a reason, they're going somewhere with this'. Recent revelations have proven all that to be false, and that leaves me with little choice but to aknowledge the shortcomings and misgivings and admit that the films are, indeed, inferior. Could a superbly written and handled next two films change my mind? I guess. The proof is always in the pudding, but I can't say I'm optimistic anymore, or expecting anything more than toybait, plotholes, and missed opportunities. All executed with far less care, skill, heart, and thought than in the original.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Tue Dec 21, 2010 7:25 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: The only way I could quell misgivings about the films and characterizations was that 'this is happening for a reason, they're going somewhere with this'. Recent revelations have proven all that to be false, and that leaves me with little choice but to aknowledge the shortcomings and misgivings and admit that the films are, indeed, inferior.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Dec 21, 2010 7:33 pm liquidus118 wrote: Could you enlighten us as to what these recent revelations are?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Dec 21, 2010 7:35 pm This is based on the interviews beginning with Enokido then Higuchi through ten pages of posts and culminating with the most recent one with Tsurumaki . I still strongly believe that these interviews need their own thread/s, one for each person interviewed. They shouldn't be buried in there just because they happen to be sourced from that book, in this humble user's repeated opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Dec 21, 2010 7:56 pm @SEELE: I can see where you are coming from, but... It's been pointed out in some form or other before, but there was apparently a lot of "making things up as we go" involved in NGE as well. If you had a chance to take a look at the Proposal or even at the drafts for EoE and ep. 24, you can probably tell that characterization, relationships, etc. (or at least their portrayal) were changed quite a bit along the way, with some elements being later "recycled" in media like the manga. That is what it looks like to me at least... NGE draft spoiler  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 21, 2010 7:58 pm Kendrix wrote: Just for lulz: Honestly, honestly think of how you would have reacted if Rebuild had come first and they had served you the original Series later. I bet you'd be calling it 'fanfic' too.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Tue Dec 21, 2010 8:02 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: This is based on the interviews beginning with Enokido then Higuchi through ten pages of posts and culminating with the most recent one with Tsurumaki . I still strongly believe that these interviews need their own thread/s, one for each person interviewed. They shouldn't be buried in there just because they happen to be sourced from that book, in this humble user's repeated opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Dec 21, 2010 8:02 pm Lili: I knew that already, but it's been 10-15 years, and they decided to come back to this. They should have done so with far more in mind. These are major motion pictures, with an already established franchise, fanbase, characters, settings, etc. There's really no excuse for coming in and pulling these sort of stunts the way they did when they were young and hungry. And of course, in addition there's the question of the quality and cohesiveness. The original had it in spades, and these new flicks sadly are lacking. All with far, far less excuse.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Dec 21, 2010 8:11 pm Just to clarify, I wasn't saying you didn't know, it just seemed worth mentioning just in case. As for quality etc... I guess it is largely a matter of opinion. I personally enjoy both NGE and NME. TBH, I personally do have a few problems with some "cohesiveness" aspects of NGE... With Rebuild, it seems everything is a "risk" in a "Damned if you do, damned if you don't" change things kind of way... compare for example reactions to 1.11 and 2.22. :EdIT: I am guessing perhaps a lot of old fans were bound to be disappointed from the moment they declared they were intent on making Rebuild more "easily accessible".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 21, 2010 8:19 pm @2.0CRC thread: Splitting that thread apart will probably be a nightmare, but I'll try to give it a look-over soon. [s:2vmh428p]Was going to put a rant here, but I think I'll save it after all.[/s:2vmh428p] Nevermind, people saw it, here it is again: Another generalized rant toward the viral bah-humbug in this subforum… I think this started out as a response of sort to Seele's misgivings, and mutated due to my current administrative preoccupation. I had a serious case of hemorrhoids over 2.XX earlier on. I can’t remember at what point I got over it and decided it was better to have fun with the new movies and not obsess so much over the things I hate. I’m sure I’ll get revved up again when the next movie comes out, thrown right back out into the tempest of THIS SHIT IS AWESOME and I FUCKING HATE YOU ANNO, but, you know what? I’ll get over it. I can’t even count the number of things in NGE I’ve had indignant fits over — (and if that sounds annoying, imagine a whole flock of me doing that; then you’ll get an idea of how I feel now ). This is fandom, and if you’re to avoid being branded as one of the accursed Fan Dumb — ever the difficult task — one thing you gotta, GOTTA do is go with the flow and manage this whole love-hate thing. Like a loved one, your beloved franchise will change. You don’t have to like all the changes, not at all. But, since there’s no way to go to counseling and attempt to make corrections where things have gone wrong, it’s all on you. Don’t get too comfortable in your trash can. Try to look at things in different ways. Understand why they are the way they are… accept it since you can’t change it… and, if you seem to really hate the films THAT much, maybe ask yourself, seriously, “Why the hell do I keep posting here?”

All executed with far less care, skill, heart, and thought than in the original.
